name: Bread in Jars: Pina Colada
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Wash 8 (1 pint) wide mouth canning jars with lids in hot, soapy water and let drain, dry, and cool to room temperature.
3. Generously prepare jars with cooking spray and flour. Drain pineapple for 10 minutes, reserving juice. Puree drained pineapple in a blender.
4. Measure out 1 1/2 cups puree, adding a little juice if necessary to make 1 1/2 cups. Set puree aside. Discard remaining juice.
5. In a mixing bowl, combine egg whites and the 1 1/2 cup of pineapple puree. Beat until light and fluffy.
6. Gradually add flour mixture (baking powder and baking soda mixed with 1 1/2 cups flour) to the pineapple mixture in thirds, beating well with each addition. Stir in shredded coconut.
7. Spoon 1 level cupful of batter into each jar. Wipe rims clean, then place jars on a baking sheet (or they'll tip over) in the center of the oven.
8. Bake for 40 minutes. Keep lids in hot water until used.
9. When cakes are done, remove jars from the oven one at a time. If rims need cleaning, use moistened paper towel.
10. Carefully put lids and rings in place, then screw tops on tightly shut. Place jars on a wire rack; they will seal as they cool.
11. Once jars are cool, decorate with round pieces of cloth and then glue on flowers, ribbons, etc., on the lid, ring, and side of the jar. Unscrew the ring (the lid should be sealed by now) and place a few cotton balls on top of the lid to make it puffy. Then place a piece of cloth about 3 inches larger than the lid and screw the ring back on. Decorate as desired.
